pi_items.source: setup_task.sh copies diagram to this location (line 22-24)
Task description explicitly requires adding 'Payment' class. Source diagram contains Customer, Order, Product classes (confirmed via XML parsing: 12 vertices, 2 edges, 0 Payment class)
Task description lines 11-13 list exactly these 4 attributes with types (int, double, Date, String). export_result.sh checks for all 4 attributes by name
Task description lines 14-15 list exactly these 2 methods: 'processPayment(): boolean' and 'refund(): void'. export_result.sh and verifier.py check for these methods

Task description line 28 requires 'There must be a new connection between Order and Payment'. Source diagram has 2 edges (Customer-Order, Order-Product). verifier.py line 102-105 requires new_connections >= 1

privileged_info_summary: The ecommerce_uml_classes.drawio diagram is a synthetic e-commerce UML class diagram containing three classes (Customer, Order, Product) with their respective attributes and methods, connected by two associations (1..* multiplicity). The task requires adding a fourth class called 'Payment' with four specific attributes (paymentId: int, amount: double, paymentDate: Date, method: String) and two methods (processPayment(): boolean, refund(): void), plus at least one association connecting the Order class to the new Payment class. The diagram file is stored at /home/ga/Diagrams/ecommerce_uml_classes.drawio. Success criteria require the Payment class to be present, at least 2 attributes visible, at least 1 new connection, and a minimum score of 65 points from the verifier.
